title: Blurry expansion predicate
exact_question: When does a blurry-named trick carry the paradox term? Footbag.org JOBs carry [PDX] where PassBack's stepping-only technical names omit it; is the paradox term physical content one source records and the other elides, or a notation convention? Either answer also settles whether each blurry-form spelling of a live canonical is an alias or a distinct paradox-bearing variant.

title: Undefined-operator definitions
exact_question: Define the structure and weight of each recognized-but-undefined folk operator: symple, slapping, frantic, fusing, phasing, slaying, sonic, twinspinning, frootie, fyro, leaning, twisted, twisting, wonton, wrecking, snapping, zipper, arctic, neutron, slicing, flapper, symp, and the bubba and alpine prefixes. Defining one operator unblocks every name that uses it.
stale_terms: barraging, illusioning, and miraging are NOT in this gate: barraging is retired into Furious, illusioning merged into atomic, miraging is historical timing language

title: Count-quantifier disposition
exact_question: How does a bare double/triple count prefix score, and are the count-quantifier names (double-dex X, triple-dex X, Double Guay and kin) dictionary rows at all? The drafted disposition routes them to internal renderer and parser cleanup rather than new trick rows.

title: Repeated same-operator scoring
exact_question: When the same scored operator appears twice in one compound (miraging ... miraging, illusioning ... illusioning), does the second occurrence add again?

title: Terraging chain arithmetic
exact_question: The registry's +3, the shipped two-dex clipper notations, footbag.org's 3-bracket terrage, and Red's ruled 4 cannot all be reconciled; which value is authoritative for the terraging chain?

title: Cross-body rake base
exact_question: What is the crossbody-rake (XBD rake) base structurally: a terminal contact posture like clipper, or a raking path? One contract; every rake compound composes defined operators onto it.

title: Path/catch terminal-encoding standard
exact_question: How is a path/catch terminal encoded in operational notation (the ambiguous-terminal framing)? A framing standard, not per-row rulings; it decides how the ambiguous-terminal formula names read.

title: Directional-syntax tokens
exact_question: Are the unresolved directional tokens in folk names (the alpine rows' inward/outward-style syntax) sanctioned path vocabulary, and how do they map onto the ratified IN/OUT terms?

title: Inward-on-a-gyro direction semantics
exact_question: What does an inward qualifier denote on a gyro, where the base carries its own OUT direction? Decides the withdrawn Inward Gyro batch.

title: Osis spin-to-catch relationship
exact_question: What is the structural relationship between the osis spin and its catch (the Butterfly near Osis hold)?

title: Sumo implicit-paradox X-Dex rider
exact_question: Does sumo's provisional [XDEX] stand (the implicit-paradox hypothesis)? Decides Nuclear Ducking Mirage and its Sumo Thong twin, whose ADD is 5 without X-Dex and 6 with it.

title: Pogo-clipper terminal
exact_question: Is the pogo-clipper terminal a kick or a stall? Row-specific to Pogo Clipper and its same-side form; pogo's +1 operator contract is registry-settled.

title: Jani-walker structure
exact_question: What is the jani-walker structure? Also decides Sidewinder (= fairy gyro jani-walker).

title: Twisting exemplar and spyro prefix
exact_question: Twisting is a degenerate structure with no exemplar (Toe Spinning Toe and the What!? name hang on it); and what does a spyro prefix mean given spyro is ruled a trick, not a general modifier?
